channel.title: Frontion — Global Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical headlines from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇨🇳🇪🇺 Surging Chinese exports threaten European economy, raising alarm at G7 summit
item.description: Eight years of US tariffs have failed to dent China industrial output, and a new wave of cheap exports is now pressuring European manufacturers, forcing G7 leaders to confront an escalating trade crisis.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇺🇦 G7 leaders open summit in France with focus on Ukraine and Middle East
item.description: World leaders face a packed agenda on the first full day of the G7 summit, with Ukrainian President Zelenskyy attending as host France pushes to refocus attention on ending the Russia-Ukraine war after the Iran ceasefire deal.

item.title: 🇪🇺💰 ECB chief Lagarde welcomes Iran ceasefire but warns inflation may persist
item.description: ECB President Christine Lagarde called the US-Iran ceasefire good news that could help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but several ECB colleagues cautioned it would not immediately bring down elevated euro zone inflation.

item.title: 🇳🇴⚖️ Norway Crown Princess son convicted of rape, sentenced to four years
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, eldest son of Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was found guilty of two counts of rape and sentenced to four years in prison in a case that has shaken the Norwegian royal family.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Iran deal offers exit from war but raises fresh political risks at home
item.description: The US-Iran peace agreement gives Trump a way out of the 3.5-month conflict, but skeptical conservatives in Congress are raising concerns over whether Iran will regain access to its frozen assets under the deal.

item.title: 🇯🇵⛏️ Takaichi pushes mineral stockpiling plan at G7, seeks to bridge divides as Japan’s Iron Lady takes the stage
item.description: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi proposed a joint critical minerals stockpiling initiative at the G7 summit to counter China’s dominance, while also welcoming the US-Iran memorandum as a step toward regional de-escalation.

item.title: 🇰🇷🇮🇩💱 South Korea and Indonesia rein in derivatives trading to defend currencies amid energy shock
item.description: Asian economies heavily reliant on imported energy are tightening oversight of speculative currency derivatives trades as the Iran war-driven oil disruption rattles forex markets.

item.title: 🇺🇦🔥 Ukrainian drone attack sets oil depot ablaze in Russia’s Krasnodar region near Crimea
item.description: A drone attack sparked a major fire at an oil depot in the village of Poltavskaya in Russia’s southern Krasnodar region, forcing the closure of a road connecting to annexed Crimea. The main fire was later extinguished.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Bank of Japan raises key interest rate to 1%, highest in three decades
item.description: The Bank of Japan raised its benchmark rate to 1%, a 31-year high, citing inflation pressures from the Middle East conflict and a weak yen. The Nikkei 225 briefly topped 70,000 for the first time before retreating.

item.title: 🇪🇺 European leaders warn Trump on Iran deal risks, push Ukraine rethink at G7 summit
item.description: European leaders told Trump at the G7 summit in Evian that a superficial interim Iran deal risks entrenching Tehran's nuclear and missile programs, while pressing him to rethink his Ukraine strategy and stand behind Kyiv.

item.title: 🇫🇷 G7 leaders convene in France awaiting clarity on US-Iran agreement terms
item.description: World leaders gathered at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains are seeking details on the US-Iran deal, with the Iran war and its implications dominating the agenda alongside trade disputes and the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: ⚽ Ebola risk for World Cup is extremely low, but US hospitals are ready, experts say
item.description: US infectious disease experts say the risk of an Ebola case arriving during the 2026 World Cup is low but not zero, and American hospitals are prepared to respond if needed amid Congo's ongoing outbreak.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil rebounds on concerns about Iran deal details and Hormuz reopening delay
item.description: Oil prices rebounded on concerns that the preliminary US-Iran peace agreement lacks detail and that resuming supply through the Strait of Hormuz could take longer than expected due to mine-clearance operations.

item.title: 🇲🇲🇨🇳 China embraces Myanmar president as former junta chief seeks legitimacy
item.description: Myanmar President Min Aung Hlaing's state visit to China gives the former junta chief a significant diplomatic boost as he seeks to consolidate power following a widely condemned election. Xi Jinping hosted the five-day visit in Beijing.

item.title: ⚽🇮🇷 Iran's US diaspora divided between protest and cheering at World Cup
item.description: Iranian Americans are torn over Iran's participation in the 2026 World Cup, with some planning to watch the game and others protesting outside the LA stadium where the team played its opening match amid the backdrop of the US-Iran war.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ How the US-Israel war with Iran unfolded over 108 turbulent days
item.description: A comprehensive timeline of the key moments in the 108-day US-Israel war with Iran that claimed thousands of lives and fundamentally reshaped the Middle East security landscape, from the first strikes to the tentative peace deal.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇪🇺 G7 leaders to meet Zelensky as Trump signals possible Ukraine breakthrough
item.description: G7 leaders will hold talks with President Zelensky on Tuesday as Trump suggested a possible breakthrough on Ukraine peace, shifting focus from the Iran deal to the ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict at the summit in France.

item.title: 📱🌍 Social media overtakes traditional outlets as worlds leading news source for first time
item.description: News consumers worldwide are increasingly turning to social media and video platforms for information, overtaking traditional outlets for the first time according to a Reuters Institute report, raising concerns about misinformation and media sustainability.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu and Trump on collision course as US-Iran deal leaves Israel sidelined
item.description: Israeli PM Netanyahu has repeatedly clashed with Trump over Israel’s refusal to constrain its pursuit of Hezbollah in Lebanon, where a cessation of hostilities is a key Iranian demand in the new US-Iran peace deal.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia slashes fuel quality rules as Ukrainian attacks squeeze domestic supplies
item.description: Russia is allowing refineries to produce lower-specification fuel for the domestic market as Ukrainian drone strikes on energy infrastructure create growing shortages, Kommersant reports.

item.title: 🇨🇩 Ebola cases top 800 in Congo as aid groups warn outbreak may be larger than reported
item.description: The outbreak is already the third largest Ebola epidemic on record and the biggest caused by the Bundibugyo strain, with aid organizations warning the true scale may exceed official figures.

item.title: 💰🌍 IMF Chief Says No Global Slowdown Yet but Risks Remain High
item.description: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva said the world economy is weathering the shock of the Middle East war with no signs yet of a global slowdown, but warned that risks remain elevated amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties.

item.title: 🇧🇾🇺🇦 Lukashenko urges both sides to compromise to end Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Belarusian President Lukashenko, one of Putin's closest allies, publicly called for compromise from both Russia and Ukraine to end the war, a notable shift in rhetoric from a key Kremlin ally.

item.title: 🇻🇪🛢️ Oil companies give Venezuela a second look amid Middle East chaos
item.description: Trump's Middle East policy disruptions are creating an unexpected beneficiary: Venezuela. Oil companies experiencing FOMO are reconsidering Venezuelan opportunities as Mideast supply uncertainties persist.

item.title: 🏛️🇺🇸 Senate Republicans demand a say on Trumps Iran deal
item.description: Republican lawmakers said they are eager to examine the fine print of the US-Iran MOU, though there is a high bar to overturning any final agreement. The move signals congressional assertiveness on war powers.
